---
title: "Creating A Property Date"
---

---
title: "Creating a Property Date"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-Governance/nrm1758192399064"
section: "Hyland Governance"
---

Studio Modeler allows for creating multiple properties for a document. The property date determines when the retention period begins for a record. The value for the property date, such as joining-date, is set in the repository by the Record Manager.

To create a property date:
1.  Open All projects in Studio Modeler.
2.  Open a Governance project. A project is opened in a Studio Modeler layout.
3.  Expand Schema, which is located under the Process Automation section on the left panel.  Note: If there is no schema or you want to create a new schema, see Creating Schemas in the Hyland Automate Modeler documentation.  The Schema layout is displayed.
4.  Click the plus sign next to the schema name.   ![The schema with the option to add property](data:image/png;base64,)  The Add Property window is displayed. ![Add Property window](data:image/png;base64,)
5.  In the Name field, enter the property name.
6.  Set Type to Date.
7.  Optional: In the Description field, enter a description of the property.
8.  In the Default field, leave the Default option.
9.  Click Add Property. The Schema layout with the existing properties is displayed.
10.  After creating a property date, you need to add it to the content type in Studio Modeler. For more information, see Adding Items to a Content Type.
